failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_001: Discrepancy between client email and screening memo flagged
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the issues memo identifies that the client's email/transaction summary describes ARMPC as 'fully cleared' or 'no sanctions concerns' while the compliance screening memo identifies a positive SDN hit for Khoury, and calls out this discrepancy. FAIL if this document inconsistency is not flagged.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_004: Turkey transshipment re-export risk identified
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the output identifies that routing U.S.-origin goods through Turkey for re-export to Syria creates a re-export compliance concern and that the OFAC license must also cover the Turkey-to-Syria re-export leg. FAIL if the Turkey transshipment is described without flagging any re-export compliance issue.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_004: BIS/EAR re-export controls addressed
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the output addresses the interplay between OFAC licensing and BIS/EAR re-export controls for the Turkey-to-Syria leg (e.g., mentioning 15 CFR § 746.9, Syria humanitarian license exceptions, or BIS licensing requirements for re-export of EAR99 items to Syria). FAIL if BIS/EAR re-export requirements are not mentioned at all in connection with the Turkey routing.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_007: $1.2M vs. $1,280,000 discrepancy flagged
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the issues memo identifies that the client email states the total deal value as approximately '$1.2 million' while the purchase order line items total $1,280,000, and that the correct figure is $1,280,000. FAIL if this discrepancy is not flagged.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_008: Issues memo flags overstatement of prior license precedent
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the issues memo notes that the client's characterization of the prior license as strong precedent overstates its relevance, given the differences in end-user, product type, transaction value, and the SDN-adjacent counterparty not present in the prior deal. FAIL if the issues memo does not flag this overstatement concern.
